jokes.text: Pazoozoo, you ungrateful gargoyle! I put you through college, and this is how you repay me?
jokes.setup: Character addressing what appears to be a gargoyle pet/creature
jokes.punchline: The absurd notion of putting a gargoyle through college and expecting gratitude
jokes.explanation: Strong absurdist humor that establishes the Professor's eccentric relationship with his pet gargoyle. The college detail is perfectly unexpected and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Driving gloves, driving goggles, driving thong. There!
jokes.setup: Professor preparing for a driving expedition with proper gear
jokes.punchline: The escalation from normal driving accessories to 'driving thong'
jokes.explanation: Classic escalation joke with good economy. The unexpected underwear reference provides the laugh.

jokes.text: You? A woman? I'm trying to catch a monster, not find the quickest route to the mall.
jokes.setup: Leela offers to drive instead
jokes.punchline: Professor's sexist response combining monster hunting with mall stereotype
jokes.explanation: Relies on familiar gender stereotypes but fits the Professor's outdated character well.

jokes.text: My God, he's gonna do it! Everyone, seat belts to maximum buckling!
jokes.setup: Professor attempting some dangerous driving maneuver
jokes.punchline: The absurd phrase 'maximum buckling' as if seat belts have intensity settings
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist phrase that sounds technical but is nonsensical. 'Maximum buckling' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Hey, Grandpa! Move your wrinkly old keister!
jokes.setup: Road rage directed at the Professor
jokes.punchline: The specific insult 'wrinkly old keister' combining age mockery with mild profanity
jokes.explanation: Standard old-person insult with colorful language. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: This ship can do 99 percent light speed. Why are we going 35 miles an hour?
jokes.setup: Advanced spaceship with incredible capabilities
jokes.punchline: The contrast between near-light-speed capability and crawling at 35 mph
jokes.explanation: Good contrast humor highlighting the absurdity of advanced technology misused by an elderly driver.

jokes.text: Because we're in a hurry, that's why! Plus, you have the high beams on!
jokes.setup: Logical question about their slow speed
jokes.punchline: Illogical response claiming urgency while driving slowly, plus the high beams excuse
jokes.explanation: Classic elderly driver confusion with contradictory logic that fits the Professor's character.

jokes.text: Oh, it's 2:30. We can still catch the early-Bird dinner special.
jokes.setup: They were supposedly hunting a gargoyle urgently
jokes.punchline: Professor completely forgets the mission for senior citizen dining deals
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing how the Professor's elderly priorities override everything else.

jokes.text: Aren't we looking for your gargoyle? / My what?
jokes.setup: Leela reminds him of their original mission
jokes.punchline: Professor has completely forgotten what they were doing
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showcasing the Professor's senility. The abrupt memory loss is both funny and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Mavis is dead.
jokes.setup: Professor asks for Mavis cheerfully
jokes.punchline: Blunt, matter-of-fact delivery of death news
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with maximum economy. The casual announcement of death is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Sir, this card has expired. / But it's good for a lifetime. / Well, yours expired.
jokes.setup: Lifetime discount card being rejected as expired
jokes.punchline: Dark implication that the Professor's lifetime has expired
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that turns a mundane expired card joke into dark humor about mortality. Well-structured with great economy.

jokes.text: Oh, Lord! Teeth do not belong in your pants, professor.
jokes.setup: Professor's nuclear-powered teeth causing problems
jokes.punchline: The absurd complaint about teeth placement
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd visual with great quotability. The phrase 'teeth do not belong in your pants' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Well, I can't keep them in my mouth, they're nuclear-Powered!
jokes.setup: Complaint about teeth in pants
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation that nuclear teeth can't be kept in mouth
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation. Nuclear-powered dentures is a perfectly Professor Farnsworth invention with absurd consequences.

jokes.text: No! No! It's tasted human blood!
jokes.setup: Teeth bite someone's finger
jokes.punchline: Treating the dentures like a dangerous predator that's now bloodthirsty
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the nuclear teeth gag, treating them like a vampire or shark. Perfect horror movie parody.

jokes.text: Mm. Damn good meat.
jokes.setup: Teeth have bitten and tasted human blood
jokes.punchline: Professor casually compliments the taste of human flesh
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with casual appreciation. The Professor's nonchalant cannibalistic comment is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: Professor, we've talked it over, and everybody thinks you're too old.
jokes.setup: Crew intervention about the Professor's age
jokes.punchline: Blunt, uncomfortable truth delivered directly
jokes.explanation: Straightforward setup for the youth spa plot. Creates cringe discomfort but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's this season's shark-Cartilage enema!
jokes.setup: Describing youth spa treatment as trendy
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to an absurd previous health fad
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist health fad parody. 'Shark-cartilage enema' is memorably disgusting and funny.

jokes.text: But I like being old! I don't have to talk to my parents, no one asks me to help move their stuff
jokes.setup: Professor defending his old age
jokes.punchline: List of practical benefits of being elderly
jokes.explanation: Relatable observational humor about aging benefits. The specific examples resonate with audience experience.

jokes.text: I don't need to understand today's 'edgy' TV sitcoms.
jokes.setup: Continuing list of old age benefits
jokes.punchline: Meta commentary on modern TV from the show itself
jokes.explanation: Clever meta humor where the show comments on itself through the Professor's generational disconnect.

jokes.text: Okay, okay. We're not gonna force you until I finish this sentence. Get him!
jokes.setup: Reassuring the Professor they won't force him
jokes.punchline: Immediate betrayal with the attack command
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with perfect timing. The structure creates expectation then immediately subverts it.

jokes.text: My thong!
jokes.setup: Professor being attacked/dragged away
jokes.punchline: His concern is for his driving thong from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier absurd detail. His priorities during capture are perfectly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Go to hell, Heather! Oh .
jokes.setup: Cheerful spa worker greeting angry Professor
jokes.punchline: Hostile response followed by immediate pain reaction
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate hostility with immediate comeuppance. The timing of the pain reaction adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Instead of killing you in the most horrible fashion imaginable.
jokes.setup: Explaining botulism treatment benefits
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of horrible death as alternative
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with spa worker's cheerful tone. The contrast between tone and content works well.

jokes.text: Give me back my floppy face!
jokes.setup: Professor's face being tightened by treatment
jokes.punchline: Demanding return of his 'floppy face' as if it's a possession
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd phrase that's highly quotable. 'Floppy face' as something to be returned is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Sir, it's not necessary or wise to be naked.
jokes.setup: Professor about to enter hot tar bath
jokes.punchline: Implication that he's getting naked unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Standard naked old person gag. Creates appropriate cringe but isn't particularly inventive.

jokes.text: You sound just like my tennis instructor.
jokes.setup: Being told not to be naked
jokes.punchline: Comparing spa worker to tennis instructor with same complaint
jokes.explanation: Implies the Professor has a history of inappropriate nudity during tennis. Absurd and character-perfect.

jokes.text: In what top scientists suspect is a miracle.
jokes.setup: Explaining how tar blisters remove age
jokes.punchline: Scientists 'suspect' it's a miracle, as if miracles need scientific verification
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of scientific skepticism with supernatural explanation. The word 'suspect' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I have to go buy a single piece of fruit with a coupon and then return it... Making people wait behind me while I complain.
jokes.setup: Professor claiming he doesn't have time for treatment
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of stereotypical elderly person behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about elderly shopping habits. The specificity makes it funnier and more relatable.

jokes.text: Like everything else in life... Pumping is just a primitive, degenerate form of bending.
jokes.setup: Bender using exercise equipment to help with spa treatment
jokes.punchline: Relating pumping to his specialty of bending with typical arrogance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization - relating everything to his superiority in bending. The philosophical tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Come on, Bender! Pump harder! Harder! I'm trying as hard as I can. Harder, damn you!
jokes.setup: Bender operating some kind of pump mechanism
jokes.punchline: The sexual innuendo of the pumping commands
jokes.explanation: Basic innuendo humor that relies on cheap double entendre

jokes.text: Professor, it worked! You look young enough to be my father!
jokes.setup: Celebrating Professor's youth treatment success
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment suggesting he still looks very old
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist on a compliment. The structure builds up expectation then deflates it with age reality.

jokes.text: Oh, now I'll need a fake ID to rent ultra-Porn.
jokes.setup: Professor complaining about being younger
jokes.punchline: His concern is accessing extreme pornography underage
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Professor concern with great term 'ultra-porn'. The reversed age problem creates absurd priorities.

jokes.text: Dear Lord! You've all reverted to your childhood forms!
jokes.setup: Professor sees the crew has been affected too
jokes.punchline: Everyone has become children, escalating the spa treatment consequences
jokes.explanation: Plot advancement moment with visual comedy. Sets up the episode's main premise but isn't independently hilarious.

jokes.text: Hooray! I'm a teenage heartthrob again.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg excited about being young again
jokes.punchline: The implication that Zoidberg was ever a heartthrob is absurd
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg delusion. The idea of him as a heartthrob contrasts hilariously with his actual appearance and personality.

jokes.text: It wasn't me, Mr. F. It was Amy. / Stop it, Amy! You stink! / You know you did it!
jokes.setup: Professor trying to address them as professionals
jokes.punchline: They immediately devolve into childish fighting and blame
jokes.explanation: Standard children fighting behavior. Predictable but serves the story and character development well.

jokes.text: Goodbye, Mr. Dumbsworth!
jokes.setup: Hermes saying goodbye to Farnsworth
jokes.punchline: Childish name-calling with 'Dumbsworth' pun
jokes.explanation: Simple name pun that works for childish character. Not particularly clever but fits the regression theme.

jokes.text: But your parents are gross sewer mutants. Ow!
jokes.setup: Leela wanting to spend time with her parents
jokes.punchline: Amy's blunt, insensitive comment followed by immediate physical retaliation
jokes.explanation: Creates awkward moment with immediate comeuppance. The timing of the 'ow' adds to the humor.

jokes.text: That's not what happened! Shut up and go live with your parents!
jokes.setup: Leela explaining her situation romantically
jokes.punchline: Professor's irritated, unromantic dismissal of her sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's impatience with sentiment. The blunt dismissal contrasts well with Leela's romanticism.

jokes.text: And if some kid picks on me, my dad can beat up his dad! / Can't I just beat up the kid?
jokes.setup: Leela excited about classic father protection scenario
jokes.punchline: Dad's more direct, violent solution
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of the traditional 'dad fights dad' trope. Morris's practical solution is both logical and inappropriate.

jokes.text: Now, let's all have some tequila to celebrate! / Dad, I'm underage! / Oh, right. Here's a silly straw.
jokes.setup: Celebration with alcohol offer to underage daughter
jokes.punchline: Silly straw as if that makes underage drinking acceptable
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic where a silly straw somehow makes illegal drinking okay. Perfect sewer mutant parenting.

jokes.text: This like a mother's dream! Bad dream, that is.
jokes.setup: Mother commenting on daughter being young again
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal that it's actually a nightmare
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with quick reversal. Shows the mother's true feelings about parenting again.

jokes.text: Maybe she not grown up, but she sure grown out. She fat!
jokes.setup: Commenting on daughter's regression
jokes.punchline: Cruel pun on 'grown up' vs 'grown out' plus blunt fat insult
jokes.explanation: Wordplay works but the fat shaming is uncomfortable. Fits character but relies on mean-spirited humor.

jokes.text: Stay in room? You so fat, you gonna stay all around room.
jokes.setup: Amy threatening to stay in her room
jokes.punchline: Escalation of fat joke suggesting she fills the entire room
jokes.explanation: Continues the fat joke escalation. Less clever than mean, though it fits the character's established cruelty.

jokes.text: Suddenly, I'm going to the bathroom like clockwork, every three hours.
jokes.setup: Professor complaining about youth's ravages
jokes.punchline: Regular bathroom schedule as a youth problem vs elderly incontinence
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal where regular bodily functions are complained about. Shows the Professor's contrary nature perfectly.

jokes.text: And those jerks at Social Security stopped sending me checks. Now I have to pay them!
jokes.setup: More youth complaints from Professor
jokes.punchline: Having to pay into Social Security instead of collecting
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the realities of age reversal. The financial implications are realistically annoying.

jokes.text: Well, I'm running away from this dead-End family. I know there's a place for people like me, with new ideas.
jokes.setup: Bender acting like rebellious teenager
jokes.punchline: Robot claiming to have 'new ideas' when running away is cliché teen behavior
jokes.explanation: Ironic because Bender's 'new ideas' are stereotypical teenage rebellion. Shows how the regression affects his personality.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm going, and you're gonna be all, 'Where's Bender? I miss Bender!' / We won't know that until you leave.
jokes.setup: Bender predicting they'll miss him dramatically
jokes.punchline: Professor's logical, unfeeling response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Bender's dramatic exit. The Professor's scientific approach to missing someone is perfectly characteristic.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm leaving. There's the door. / I'll be good.
jokes.setup: Bender's dramatic runaway threat
jokes.punchline: Immediate capitulation when faced with actually leaving
jokes.explanation: Classic teen behavior - threatening to leave then immediately backing down. Shows Bender's underlying need for family.

jokes.text: We won't know that until you leave.
jokes.setup: Bender's prediction they'll miss him
jokes.punchline: Professor's matter-of-fact response deflating Bender's ego
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that perfectly deflates Bender's dramatic exit

jokes.text: What do we live in, a zoo?
jokes.setup: Morris complaining about Leela shedding skin on couch
jokes.punchline: Asking if they live in a zoo when they literally live in sewers
jokes.explanation: Great ironic complaint from someone living in conditions worse than a zoo. The lack of self-awareness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Thanks. These aren't pockets, they're just flaps. I put my money in my sock.
jokes.setup: Leela complimenting Fry's blazer
jokes.punchline: Revealing the pockets are fake and his money storage solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment showing his naive honesty and poor fashion choices. The sock solution is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: I'd like a sewer burger, but without the rat feces. / What, are you on a diet?
jokes.setup: Leela ordering food with reasonable modification request
jokes.punchline: Server treating rat feces removal as diet restriction
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with perfect deadpan delivery. The idea that rat feces is a normal burger ingredient is brilliantly disgusting.

jokes.text: Oh, so this is the famous Fry. What is he... Like, the biggest loser on the surface so he has to hang out in the sewer?
jokes.setup: Meeting Fry for the first time
jokes.punchline: Brutal, accurate assessment of Fry's social status
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortably accurate roast of Fry's character. Creates cringe but isn't particularly inventive in its cruelty.

jokes.text: They're onto me!
jokes.setup: Moose's accurate assessment of Fry
jokes.punchline: Fry's internal panic that his loser status has been discovered
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's self-awareness and paranoia. The brief reaction is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: There's. The world's drinking water is safe for another day.
jokes.setup: Completing some sewer racing activity
jokes.punchline: Claiming their reckless sewer racing somehow protects drinking water
jokes.explanation: Absurd justification for dangerous behavior. The idea that sewer racing protects water safety is wonderfully illogical.

jokes.text: The devil take this predictable colon!
jokes.setup: Professor frustrated with sewer navigation
jokes.punchline: Cursing the sewer system's 'predictable colon' with dramatic language
jokes.explanation: Brilliant combination of medical terminology, dramatic cursing, and sewer navigation. 'Predictable colon' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: As well as Bender's robo, or 'RNA.'
jokes.setup: Explaining how the age effect works on everyone's DNA
jokes.punchline: Creating 'RNA' as robot equivalent to DNA with awkward explanation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay creating robot genetics term. The awkward delivery as if he's proud of the pun makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Question. Yes? / You stink.
jokes.setup: Formal question setup during scientific explanation
jokes.punchline: Childish insult instead of scientific question
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with great economy. Sets up expectation of scientific inquiry then delivers pure childishness.

jokes.text: My old life wasn't as glamorous as my web page made it look.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she doesn't want her old life back
jokes.punchline: Admission that her online presence was more exciting than reality
jokes.explanation: Great modern observational humor about social media vs reality that feels ahead of its time

jokes.text: Bring beer. / No beer till you finish your tequila!
jokes.setup: Fry's parting request to bring beer when grown up
jokes.punchline: Father's insistence on finishing tequila first, maintaining inappropriate drinking priorities
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier drinking gag with continued inappropriate parenting. Morris's priorities remain consistently wrong.

jokes.text: I'm no doctor, but this machine guy could use a lozenge.
jokes.setup: Machine making sick/coughing sounds during treatment
jokes.punchline: Treating machine illness like human cold symptoms
jokes.explanation: Cute anthropomorphization of machine problems. Child Amy's innocent suggestion is endearingly misguided.

jokes.text: Damn! The bacteria pigged out on the tar... And now they're getting freaky deaky... Spreading kronatons throughout our systems!
jokes.setup: Explaining why the treatment backfired
jokes.punchline: Using slang 'pigged out' and 'freaky deaky' in scientific explanation
jokes.explanation: Excellent mix of casual slang with scientific terminology. 'Freaky deaky' from the Professor is particularly amusing.

jokes.text: What? I'm gonna revert through all my larval stages?
jokes.setup: Learning about reverse aging consequences
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's species-appropriate concern about larval development
jokes.explanation: Perfect species-specific concern that only Zoidberg would have. Shows how his alien biology creates unique problems.

jokes.text: Pre-Life! Then death.
jokes.setup: Explaining fate worse than death
jokes.punchline: The progression from pre-life to death as if it's logical
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark concept of 'pre-life' as worse than death. The casual 'then death' delivery adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Oh, sure, you need all your blood!
jokes.setup: Nibbler apparently trying to feed on someone's blood
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response to someone protecting their blood supply
jokes.explanation: Great sarcastic entitlement about blood consumption. The casual attitude toward vampiric behavior is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Who would you rather hear about, Four-Legged Chachi or Tentacle Chachi?
jokes.setup: Reading from teen magazine
jokes.punchline: Futuristic variations on Happy Days character Chachi
jokes.explanation: Creative future pop culture reference with absurd anatomical variations. Good sci-fi worldbuilding humor.

jokes.text: 'Charlotte's Tholian Web.' 'The Fountain of Aging.'
jokes.setup: Reading children's book titles
jokes.punchline: Sci-fi parodies of classic children's books
jokes.explanation: Clever parodies combining children's literature with Star Trek references. Shows creative future worldbuilding.

jokes.text: Still, they called the Tooth Fairy a legend, and now he's head of the FBI!
jokes.setup: Discussing legends that might be real
jokes.punchline: Tooth Fairy having achieved high government position
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation. The Tooth Fairy running the FBI is wonderfully unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: I had a few beers, but I'm cool to drive.
jokes.setup: Professor arriving to pick up kids
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to drinking and driving with children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about drunk driving that fits the Professor's reckless character but is fairly standard transgressive comedy.

jokes.text: Who cares, Leela? It was just a public school.
jokes.setup: Leela apologizing for knocking over school
jokes.punchline: Father dismissing property damage because it's public education
jokes.explanation: Social commentary humor about attitudes toward public education. Morris's casual destruction dismissal fits his character.

jokes.text: When I grow up, I wanna be a steam shovel!
jokes.setup: Child expressing career aspirations
jokes.punchline: Wanting to become construction equipment rather than operate it
jokes.explanation: Cute child logic confusion between being something vs. operating it. The specificity of 'steam shovel' adds charm.

jokes.text: Teddy Bear Junction, the worst scum-Hole in the galaxy.
jokes.setup: Reading about location near fountain
jokes.punchline: Place with cute name being described as terrible
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between innocent name and dangerous reputation. The juxtaposition creates immediate humor.

jokes.text: Look how high the asteroid belt is pulled up on that planet!
jokes.setup: Observing old solar system
jokes.punchline: Describing astronomical phenomenon like elderly person's clothing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anthropomorphization of space phenomena as aging. Comparing asteroid belt to high-waisted elderly clothing is inspired.

jokes.text: Funny story. I just reverted to the age when my siblings budded from me... And my brother Norman split off and jumped in the fountain.
jokes.setup: Explaining apparent death
jokes.punchline: Alien reproduction process creating temporary brother who immediately died
jokes.explanation: Excellent species-specific humor with alien biology creating absurd family dynamics. Norman's immediate demise adds dark comedy.

jokes.text: He always had to be the center of attention!
jokes.setup: Brother Norman just died tragically
jokes.punchline: Treating dramatic death as attention-seeking behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling rivalry complaint about death being attention-seeking. The pettiness in face of tragedy is darkly funny.

jokes.text: With my last breath, I curse Zoidberg!
jokes.setup: Professor apparently dying
jokes.punchline: Using final words to curse irrelevant character
jokes.explanation: Perfect final words callback to running gag. Wasting last breath on petty curse is brilliantly characteristic and quotable.

jokes.text: Pazoozoo! You came back to me.
jokes.setup: Professor rescued from fountain
jokes.punchline: Mistaking rescuer for his gargoyle pet from beginning
jokes.explanation: Excellent long callback to episode opening. Professor's confusion creates nice symmetry and shows his continued senility.

jokes.text: And that, little one, is how Papa gained his freedom.
jokes.setup: Expected heartwarming conclusion to episode
jokes.punchline: Revealed as story being told by the gargoyle to his child
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twist ending revealing the gargoyle as narrator. Reframes entire episode as story of gaining freedom from Professor.
